name: Lona Trading MCP Server (agency.lona)
what_it_does: AI-powered trading strategy development MCP server — providing backtesting, market data access, and portfolio analysis tools. Enables AI agents to develop and test trading strategies, analyze market data, and evaluate portfolio performance via a streamable HTTP remote MCP endpoint.
use_cases: Developing and backtesting trading strategies from quantitative finance agents
Retrieving and analyzing market data for AI-driven investment research agents
Running portfolio analysis and performance attribution from wealth management agents
Building automated trading research workflows with AI strategy development
not_for: Live trade execution (strategy development and backtesting focus)
Retail investors without quantitative finance background
best_when: A quantitative analyst or trading team wants AI agents to assist in developing and backtesting trading strategies — combining AI reasoning with historical market data and portfolio analytics.
avoid_when: You want live order execution. FINANCIAL RISK: backtesting results don't guarantee future performance. Strategy-related decisions require human review.

auth.notes: API key required for Lona service. Streamable HTTP remote endpoint. Review data licensing terms for market data access.

pricing.notes: Pricing not publicly documented. Commercial service with market data — data licensing costs likely.

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: Trading strategy outputs may influence real investment decisions
Backtesting results do not predict future performance — agents must caveat outputs
Market data licensing: verify data terms of service for commercial use
Remote MCP endpoint availability depends on Lona service uptime
Limited public documentation — verify capabilities before building agent workflows
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: Trading data influences financial decisions. Market data licensing. Remote endpoint security. API key protection essential.

security_score_components.security_notes: Trading data with financial risk implications. API key security required. Data licensing compliance. HTTPS enforced on remote endpoint.
